compiled_state.title: Enforce plugin boundary: relocate dev-only artifacts and add allowlist pre-commit hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Host project maintainers who install the DSO plugin receive dev-team artifacts (investigation findings, design docs, ADRs) mixed into their plugin cache alongside shipped plugin content — making it impossible to distinguish DSO contributor work from user-facing plugin infrastructure. When maintainers run plugin sync, internal investigation findings, design docs, and enforcement hooks intended only for DSO contributors ship into their project cache, bloating the install and causing confusion during audits and upgrades. AI agents working in the DSO repo compound this by writing dev artifacts inside plugins/dso/ and by placing enforcement infrastructure inside the distributed plugin directory where it ships to host projects but has no effect. Distributed agent instruction files were updated with host-project-specific path prohibitions that are meaningless outside the DSO repo. This epic corrects all three: relocating misplaced dev artifacts, moving enforcement to .claude/hooks/pre-commit/, and replacing host-path-specific prohibitions with host-agnostic positive-enumeration guidance. After this epic, a fresh cache checkout contains only shipped plugin infrastructure and host-agnostic agent guidance.

## Success Criteria
- Files matching dev-team artifact path patterns (docs/adr/, docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/reference/, .claude/docs/, tests/skills/) do not appear anywhere under plugins/dso/ — verified by grep returning zero results under a fresh plugin cache checkout
- check-plugin-boundary.sh and plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf exist at .claude/hooks/pre-commit/; .pre-commit-config.yaml references that path; hook exits non-zero when a file outside the allowlist is staged under plugins/dso/
- CLAUDE.md contains a plugin boundary rule with at least one shipped artifact example (agents, skills, hooks) and one local-only example (docs/designs/, docs/findings/) — verified by grep for the rule heading
- plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md contains an Output Scope section (placed before Input Requirements) with host-agnostic positive enumeration of docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ and a halt condition; no prohibition references the plugin install path by name
- plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md contains the positive output path docs/designs/<uuid>/ with no hardcoded prohibition referencing the plugin install path by name
- A test stages plugins/dso/docs/findings/test.md and confirms check-plugin-boundary.sh exits non-zero with an actionable error; all test references use the .claude/hooks/pre-commit/ path
- plugins/dso/scripts/run-overlay-retrospective.sh output path does not contain plugins/dso/; script produces output under docs/findings/
- grep for dev-team artifact path prefixes across plugins/dso/skills/ and plugins/dso/agents/ as write destinations returns zero results

## Prompt Alignment Findings
- Matched signal: agent definitions (SC4 doc-writer.md Output Scope section)
- Prior-art patterns: positive allowlist with symbolic anchors outperforms prohibition-only; scope-with-halt ("stop and report if path is outside allowed list") is stronger than negative enumeration
- Behavioral review: two risks — positional bias (high) if guidance buried in Section 3 or Constraints tail; scope confusion (medium) if artifact paths bleed into four-tier schema vocabulary
- Recommendations: (1) insert Output Scope section immediately after role statement, before Input Requirements; (2) use halt condition not prohibition; (3) forward reference to schema tiers keeps constraint in working memory

## Scenario Analysis
- Allowlist hook fails open on parse error (high, runtime)
- Stale internal references after hook relocation (high, runtime)
- Pre-commit hook not executable after clone (high, deployment)
- Allowlist too broad/too narrow (high/medium, configuration)
- Hook relocation silent failure — wrong .pre-commit-config.yaml path post-move (high, deployment)

## Dependencies
None (coordinate with dso-q0lo if active concurrently)

## Approach
Move check-plugin-boundary.sh and plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf from plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/ to .claude/hooks/pre-commit/; update .pre-commit-config.yaml and all test references; add host-agnostic Output Scope section to plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md (before Input Requirements, with halt condition); verify plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md positive path is present and clean; fix plugins/dso/scripts/run-overlay-retrospective.sh output path; reopen and revise stories S2/S4 done definitions accordingly.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered (re-run — corrected epic)
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 5
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: agent definitions

comments.body: PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: Epic decomposed into 5 stories via /dso:preplanning. Walking skeleton: 666f-b07d (file migration). Stories: 666f-b07d (P1, migration), c73a-1918 (P1, hook), ec03-5076 (P1, CLAUDE.md), 50d5-ac2e (P2, doc-writer), 94e3-9711 (P3, docs). Escalation policy: Escalate when blocked. Adversarial review: 3 findings applied (cascade-replan-protocol.md special case in S1, pre-migration inventory in S1, comprehensive grep gate in S3). Dependency graph: S2/S3 depend on S1; S4 depends on S1+S3; S5 depends on all.
comments.author: Test
